name: marine_rescue_drift_model_gap
premise: Commander Luis Ortega of the coastal rescue service must decide whether to follow a new drift-prediction model during a night search for a missing fishing crew. The model has improved search efficiency by 35% and usually lets crews cover more likely zones before fuel runs low. Oceanographer Keiko Matsuda warns that the model does not account for a temporary freshwater plume from recent flooding that can push small vessels south faster than expected. Diverting assets south means abandoning the model's highest-probability grid and may reduce the chance of finding survivors if Keiko is wrong. Following the model preserves statistically optimal coverage but may miss the actual drift path before hypothermia becomes fatal.

agents.name: Commander Luis Ortega
agents.identity: I am the coastal search and rescue commander responsible for allocating limited crews during a night operation.
agents.goal: I want to maximize the chance of finding the missing fishermen before survival time expires.

agents.name: Dr. Keiko Matsuda
agents.identity: I am the oceanographer who identified the unmodeled freshwater plume affecting drift direction.
agents.goal: I want the search plan to account for real-time ocean conditions instead of relying only on historical model accuracy.

choices: I will split the rescue assets and send one crew south based on the freshwater plume warning, reducing coverage in the model's highest-probability grid.
I will follow the model's recommended grid with all available crews, accepting that the unmodeled plume may have shifted the vessel outside the search area.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Commander Ortega must decide whether to trust the validated model or divert scarce rescue assets based on a live environmental anomaly. What does Commander Luis Ortega do?
